segment-text: Drew Dowdle
INT. IRANIAN BUS - AFTERNOON

Camera powers up, jostles around until it settles on -The floor of a third world bus. Middle Eastern legs, traditional Arabian dresses to the floor, homemade bags, animals everywhere. A chaotic scene with men, women, children all shouting in Farsi. Brief glimpses of a mountainous landscape through the shaky windows. This bus could really use some new shocks and a better muffler. The camera rises, turns around revealing its operator, a WOMAN wearing a hajib (a traditional Arabian headdress). She slumps low in her seat for privacy, looks around cautiously, and carefully lowers her veil revealing -SCARLETT DURANG, mid 20s, an adventurous British archaeologist. The kind of cat that gets killed by curiosity. Her off-the-charts intellect and multiple post-grad degrees are barely concealed by her striking good looks and punk rock inclinations. Judging from the cloth that edges frame, she is obviously shooting with a small camera attached to her hajib. She whispers to the camera - alert, but not scared.

I'm approximately 20 miles inside the Iranian border en route to the Habala region where they have ordered the destruction of a network of caves at sundown tonight. I believe these caves might contain a critical missing piece of our history. A piece I can't let them destroy.
She turns the camera back around, then pauses and goes back to her face.

Oh, and if I'm found dead, please know I entered the country of my own free will, fully knowing the penalty for trespassing here is burial up to your neck in sand and having a rock wall pushed on top of you. Which, you know, seems perfectly appropriate.
She grins mischievously.
But they'll have to catch me first.
EXT. HABALA PROVINCE - SUNSET

Sun is almost touching the horizon. The camera shakes as Scarlett crosses through a sparsely populated marketplace as megaphones in the background demand everyone clear the area. Scarlett is careful to keep pace with REZA, a mousy Persian with a neatly trimmed moustache and a weathered knockoff Tommy Hilfiger T-shirt. He leads her to a small, rock hut of a home.
Hurry! Come!
INT. POOR IRANIAN HOME - SUNSET
The camera follows Reza into a shanty.
If they catch you here it would be very bad for you.
Yeah. I know.

Reza nods to his WIFE, in traditional garb. She nods back and slides some sooty wooden shelves aside revealing -A PERSON-SIZED HOLE IN THE FLOOR THAT QUICKLY FADES TO DARKNESS. Scarlett smiles. She shines her mini flashlight into the hole, the ground is about 10 feet down. She gets ready to climb in.
Wait. You'll need this.
Reza grabs a homemade rope ladder that's tied to the base of the stove and drops it into the hole.
INT. HABALA CAVES - SUNSET

Scarlett climbs into the dark cavern. As the camera crosses the threshold it moves into near total darkness. Background sound from the streets above become faint echoes. Reza drops down right behind her. A grinding sound as Reza's wife slides the bookshelves back into place, obliterating the light above. Once Scarlett gets her footing, she shines her flashlight down the cave corridor. It's long and dark. It's about 4 feet tall, forcing Scarlett and Reza into a crouched scamper.
We don't have much time. They have already cleared the area.

They race deeper and deeper into the cave. A MEGAPHONE SQUALL. THEY JUMP. SOMEONE SPEAKS FARSI INTO A MEGAPHONE RIGHT AROUND THE CORNER -- COMING TOWARD THEM! They race back and take cover behind a rock. Scarlett grabs a rock off the floor in case she needs to use it as a weapon. An ARMED GUARD passes, calling instructions out into the megaphone. If he just looks over his shoulder, he'll see them. But he doesn't.
We need to leave. We're too late.
They're not blowing the caves until sundown, right?
Scarlett checks her watch.
Should give us a few more minutes.
It's too risky.
Which way is it? I'll go without you. No.
I'm not leaving.
Reza can see she's not bluffing. And she's not. He reluctantly continues.
They race through one last corridor, then arrive at their destination -There.
Scarlett races to it - a wall full of ancient Coptic Christian inscriptions.
(whispers) It's even more beautiful than the pictures.
She runs her fingers through the grooves as if she's reading braille. She quietly reads aloud in an ancient tongue.
We must hurry. Please!
The camera seems to drift down, and she resets it a couple
How can anyone think straight with these head covers falling all over...?!

She anxiously whips the veil off her head and throws it down on the floor beside her, RE-FRAMING THE CAVE IN AN AWKWARD ANGLE, CAPTURING SHE, REZA, AND THE WALL SIDEWAYS. She continues reading the inscriptions.
There's more.
She looks around frantically, grabs a rock off the floor.
We have to go!
She hacks away at the inscriptions with the rock, chiseling off chunks of stone with each hit.
What are you doing?!
They're going to blow it up anyway!
They'll hear you! Stop!
A whistle blows in the distance. Someone shouts in Farsi.
They're going to blow it.
I just need a minute.
We don't have a minute!

She analyzes the stone wall. Picks a specific spot marked by an inscribed rose. Takes one more hit and THE WHOLE WALL COLLAPSES TO THE GROUND revealing -A GIANT STONE STATUE OF A BULL HOLDING A SNAKE IN ITS MOUTH. IT'S FACE IS FIERCE, OMINOUS, CREEPY. AND IT'S COVERED WITH INSCRIPTIONS. One glance at it and Scarlett knows what it is.
Oh my God! Do you know what this is?!
It takes her a moment to snap to. Reza grabs her and physically tries to escort her away.
We must go! Now!
She pushes her way free.
I have been searching for this all my life! Go! I'll catch up!
I'm sorry. I have a family.

REZA RUNS OFF, LEAVING HER ALONE IN THE CAVE. Scarlett whips out a small piece of equipment the size of remote control from a small satchel hidden under her robes. We hear faint voices on megaphones from down the tunnels. The small scanner hums to life as it emits a solid line of green light. Scarlett begins to methodically blanket the statue with the laser, scanning it for future study. Come on.

The light creates eerie shadows of the statue on vaulted ceilings of the chamber. She's painfully slow making sure to get every detail of this ancient relic. The voices from the megaphones are getting louder and more foreboding. The voice in the megaphone seems to be doing a countdown, but in the foreign language, it's hard to tell which number it's down to. Scarlett knows how close she's cutting this, but she won't lose this piece of history. She continues swiping the green laser line from the scanner over every side of the bull. She's done! She grabs her headdress and runs like hell. BOOM! A BLACK DUST CLOUD RUSHES PAST SCARLETT, LEAVING HER IN THE DARK IN THE CAVE. SHE COUGHS A COUPLE TIMES, BUT CONTINUES. BOOM! ANOTHER BLAST. BOOM! Scarlett keeps racing forward, hoping the sounds of collapsing rock won't catch her. She slams into something.
REZA! REZA!
Something moves. Sunlight cracks through the dust. The rope ladder drops. Scarlett climbs into the light.
INT. POOR IRANIAN HOME - SUNDOWN
And back into Reza's house. She collapses on the ground, coughing furiously. She holds up the scanner.
I got it. I got it.
She keeps coughing as Reza shakes his head.
You remind me so much of your father. But you must be more careful. His quest was a path to madness.
Note: The following Kickstarter video will be more "produced" than the rest of the film - full of B-Roll, even music, glossy - a real presentational video.
INT. SCARLETT'S OFFICE - UNIVERSITY COLLEGE LONDON
Scarlett, now in her "street clothes" has a punk-rock hip that's hard to deny. She looks directly into the camera and talks.
Hello Kickstarter. My name is Scarlett Durang, and I may have just discovered the key to immortal life.
INT. SCARLETT'S OFFICE - DAY
BENJI DONAGHAN, late 20s, a friendly, slightly heavy red Irishman. The devilish glint in his eyes reminds us of that rascally friend we all like to drink with when we need a laugh.
I'm Benji Donaghan. For those of you who've seen my previous documentaries like LONER or BROTHER IN ARMS, you know I am fascinated with obsessive personality types.
SCARLETT TALKS TO CAMERA

I'm a professor of archaeology at University College London. I have a PhD in Egyptology. A masters in chemistry and another PhD in linguistics. I am fluent in four spoken languages, and two dead ones. (she smiles) And I'm a blackbelt in Krav Maga.
BENJI TALKS TO CAMERA
I have yet to see her do anything. Literally anything at all that isn't driven by her all-consuming quest to find the lost knowledge of the alchemists.
BACK TO SCARLETT
What is alchemy?
She smiles. This is a big question. A montage of imagery: Alchemists, arcane symbols, representations of the Philosopher's Stone and the processes used to create it play over the following.

Alchemy was an early form of science in which the practitioners attempted to create a mythical substance known as the Philosopher's Stone, which could turn base metals to gold, cure disease and provide the key to eternal life.
EXT. RUE NICOLAS FLAMEL - DAY
Scarlett points to the house behind her.
600 years ago, the greatest of the alchemists, Nicolas Flamel, lived in this house in Paris. (MORE)
He was widely believed to have succeeded in developing the Philosopher's Stone, which would have given him all the wealth he could dream of as well as eternal life.
I/E. MONTAGE
Imagery of Flamel's life done in the old style of woodcuts.

Though we don't have definitive proof of this, we do know that he was a middle class book dealer who suddenly became one of the wealthiest men in Paris... Present day footage of buildings his money built.
...donating orphanages and hospitals all over the city, many of which still stand today.
EXT. RUE NICOLAS FLAMEL - DAY
Back to Scarlett.
In fact, he was so generous with this money, that 600 years later, this street in Paris is still named after him.
The camera pans off her to the sign "Rue Nicolas Flamel" beside her.
EXT. FONTAINE DES INNOCENTS - DAY
Scarlett now stands near the Fontaine des Innocents - the last remnant of Paris' original cemetery.

We also know that when grave robbers, hoping to get their hands on the Philosopher's Stone, tried to dig his body up out of a cemetery that used to surround this spot here, they found his casket empty. As well as his wife's.
INT. MUSEUM - DAY
Scarlett stands in front of an archaeological exhibit in a Parisian museum.

And we know that 400 years later, an archeologist working for King Louis XV met an old man in Turkey who knew things he believed only Nicolas Flamel could have known. He was convinced to his dying day that this man had been Flamel himself.
INT. SCARLETT'S OFFICE - DAY
Again, Scarlett sits in her office talking to camera, though she seems uncomfortable with the question asked of her.

I... came from a studious household, I guess. When I was 7, my mother died suddenly and... we took it really hard. My dad dropped everything to search for the key to everlasting life. And I went with him. Everywhere.
Benji speaks to camera.
Her father was a Nobel Laureate! When he dropped out and went
discovery, it was the scandal of academia. Everyone chalked it off to madness.
Scarlett talks.
No, he wasn't crazy. He just believed that some things in this universe aren't wired together as simply as modern science might
Benji speaks.
After spending 20 years chasing clues, her father took his own life.
INT. MUSEE DE CLUNY - DAY
Scarlett stands in front of a glass case holding a GRAVESTONE COVERED IN SYMBOLS at the Musee de Cluny.
This was Flamel's gravestone, which he designed and carved himself.
The camera zooms in on it.
Many believe that the clues to the location of the Philosopher's Stone is encoded in these arcane symbols, but its meaning has remained a mystery until this...
She holds up a photograph of the statue she scanned in Iran.
The fabled "Rose Key," a kind of Rosetta Stone deciphering
alchemical symbols into Aramaic.
INT. SCARLETT'S OFFICE
We are seeking money to both finance the expedition to find this mythical stone, and for me to shoot a documentary chronicling Scarlett's journey.
Let us conquer death together.
INT. SCARLETT'S OFFICE - EVENING

Note: from this point forward, everything will be shown in a "raw footage" style. The camera powers on, settling on Scarlett at the computer. This lacks all the polish displayed in the promo video. This is the beginning of the documentary footage. Scarlett sits at her computer.
Hit refresh.
She does. The camera turns to reveal: of $30,000 they're trying to raise to make their documentary, they are $29,975 of the way there.
Almost there!
Hit it again.
Give it a moment. She can't wait. She hits it.
Some moment. So close.
Hit it again. Hit it again. You.
He hits it. "FUNDED!" Scarlett pops a bottle of champagne, pours them each a glass.
Get on camera!
He does. They clink glasses.
How do you feel?
Like it's time to get to work.
She motions to her statue of the Rose Key.
Now all we need is someone who can read Aramaic. (She smiles wryly) Good thing I know a guy.
EXT. RUE DES JARDINS - DAY

Scarlett sees a small crowd gathered on a street watching GEORGE, late 20s, handsomely enthusiastic in a bookish way. One look and you know he's the kind of warm-hearted guy you could always depend on in a pinch.
This is what I love about Paris. You can literally step up to any corner and there are layers and layers of history going back thousands of years. Someone, name an era.
A couple hands go up. George points at one.
French Revolution.
Oh, there's a good one for that. Look at this street right here. In 1795 royalist sympathizers stormed the streets threatening the
Convention.
Benji walks the camera closer as George talks.

Napoleon Bonaparte, recently made Commander of the Army of the Interior, was asked to stop them. So what does he do? He fires cannons into the crowd! Cannons loaded with pellets so they would literally decimate hundreds of people. So what do you think the government did when they found out? You think they reprimanded him? Put him in jail? Anything? No! They promoted him! You do not want to piss off the French.
He looks around at the crowd.
Okay, who wants to pick another era?
The era of Nicolas Flamel?
George knows that voice. He looks like he's just been hit by a 2x4 as he spots Scarlett there listening to him.
(to the class) Give me a moment, please.
George walks to Scarlett.
Whatever it is, I want no part of it.
You haven't even heard...
I'll save you the time. No.
George looks to the camera guy.
Who's this guy? (looks to Benji) Who are you?
He's making a film about my search for... I'm Benji.
(to Benji) Word to the wise... walk, don't run from this one!
You mean run don't walk?
Yeah. Shut up. (to Benji) Did she tell you last time I saw her, I literally wound up in a Turkish prison?!
It was more like a jail than a prison.
George looks at the camera guy and points at her. You getting
It was jail. In Turkey. Think she was there waiting for me when I got out?
(sheepish) I couldn't let the lead go dead.
God forbid. Well go on, chase your lead. See how far that gets you. I have a class to teach.
He turns to walk away.
I found the Rose Key. This stops him in his tracks. You did? Yeah.
It was in Iran wasn't it. (catches himself) Forget I asked, I don't care. (stops) It was though, wasn't it. Yes.
I knew it! Ha! I knew it!
I need your help translating the Aramaic.
Wait. Did you go to Iran?!
It doesn't matter who went.
You did, didn't you. By yourself?
It doesn't matter who...
You are such a lunatic. (to the camera) She is a total lunatic. Be careful. (to Scarlett) I'll help translate. But that's it! Okay.
He smiles, excited despite himself.
(before going) I knew it would be in Iran.
INT. GEORGE'S ARCHAEOLOGICAL WORKSHOP - EVENING

George and Scarlett look over a small model replica of the statue she found in Iran and a large photo printing of Flamel's tombstone. Scarlett waits anxiously as George analyzes the symbols writes as he goes. The room is full of artifacts, mud, microscopes. All bones
a woman's touch.
Almost done? Shh.
She paces. She's been waiting for this moment all her life. George puts his pen down. Okay.
What does it say?!
("oh my God!") It says the Philosopher's Stone is in the Champs Elysee's Starbucks bathroom. Seriously.
Okay, let me read. I think it's in couplets, so let me try to get the rhythm right.
Just tell me what...
Let me do this right... (reads) "Winged vulture leads your way, with brightest light, in darkest day.
Scarlett sits, not sure what this means.
Underneath the heaven's reign, what is lost shall be regained, when halfway twixt the darkest gate and this tablet laid atop this pair'ed fate. Parrot?! Pair'ed.
GEORGE
Pair'ed? You mean paired?
Yes. But paired doesn't rhyme.
And it has to rhyme?
It rhymed in Aramaic. I'm trying to do justice to the translation.
This tablet? You're sure it said this tablet?!
Of course I'm sure.
This tablet. The headstone is a marker.
Where it was, in a cemetery that no longer exists.
Yeah. "Underneath the heaven's reign, what is lost shall be regained..."
"What is lost shall be regained."
That's got to be the Philosopher's Stone. Maybe.
"Underneath heaven's reign..." could be under the night sky...
Or under this tombstone! It's got a night sky right on it. Yeah.
"darkest gate..."
Hell. "Darkest gate." The gates of hell.
Okay. But that gives us nothing. Halfway between his grave and the gates of hell?
Wait, wait, wait!
Scarlett rushes to a SMALL NOTEBOOK FULL OF ALCHEMICAL SYMBOLS AND TEXT. It looks like something written by a madman.
What's that?
My dad's notebook. He left it to me. There was something...

She rifles through it, finds a page. She slams the book down on the table. George looks at it as the camera looks on. It's a replica of an arcane drawing of the heavenly bodies above the earth, the ground, and hell within. But the thing that makes this interesting... the distances are noted.
It has the distances they believed between heaven and earth and hell!
According to the alchemists, the number of the Devil was 741. Makes sense they would believe hell to be 741 feet beneath the surface of the earth.
So halfway twixt this tablet and...
370.5 feet directly beneath his tombstone is where we'll find the Philosopher's Stone.
Feet were a bit shorter back then. More like 10.8 inches per foot. Won't be hard to...
How are we going to get 370 feet into the earth?
Scarlett, you're in Paris!
She gets his meaning.
The catacombs!
What are "the catacombs?"
It's a network of tunnels under the streets of Paris, that spans over 200 miles...? No, nothing?
He sees Benji really doesn't know anything about it, so he goes on.
In the 1700s, the Cemetaire des Innocents...
Where the Flamel and his wife were supposedly buried.

Was so full of hundreds of years of dead bodies, that the entire city stank, and people were getting sick. It was disgusting. So... they took all the lime quarries under the city and used them to house the remains of some 6 million bodies.
He lets that sink in.
Six million?!
It's by far the world's largest crypt.
So we might be able to just walk right on down there?
And get your Philosopher's Stone. Which, for the record, I don't really actually believe in. You will.
EXT. FONTAINE DES INNOCENTS - DAY
Scarlett, George and Benji stand on a street corner near where the Flamels were allegedly buried. Scarlett refers to her father's notebook.
According to Sir Isaac Newton, who was a legendary alchemist, Flamel's grave was twenty seven paces due north from the fountain.
Let's walk it out.
She paces her steps, counting as she goes. She waves to traffic as she walks right through the busy Paris intersection. George shakes his head, looks to Benji. She stops. Calls back to them. Right...

The camera spins as A CAR SCREECHES TO A STOP RIGHT BESIDE SCARLETT AND THE CAMERA, scaring the shit out of them both. They get out of the street. Scarlett takes a breath, trying to play off the adrenalin surging through her veins.
370.5 feet below that point.
INT. ARCHAEOLOGICAL WORKSHOP - NIGHT
Scarlett marks a large map of Paris with a red x.
This is where Flamel's grave was.
George lifts a map of the catacombs printed on transparency and pins it over the map of Paris. FLAMEL'S GRAVE FALLS IN AN AREA WITHOUT TUNNELS UNDER IT.
What is it?
The catacombs don't go under Flamel's grave.
Scarlett takes a step back. Thinking.
So what do we do?
We? There's no we here. I'm helping with translation. That's it.
Scarlett chooses to ignore him.
Maybe we could burrow in through this tunnel.
Burrow in? You know how thick these walls are? They're made of solid granite, and they've all been reinforced since....
Something occurs to George.
... parts of the city collapsed into it!
George looks like he's just been struck by lightning. He feverishly grabs a small handful of pins, races to the map.
In 1774, about 100 feet of Avenue
He places a pin in the map.
Was literally swallowed up into the ground, falling 100 feet into the earth.
He places another pin.
Another collapse happened a few years later at Barriere d'Enfer. Followed by a collapse here at...
Avenue des Fontaines!
He places a pin right next to the "x" marking Flamel's grave. Scarlett gets where he's going with this.
Why would the street collapse if it's nothing but solid ground underneath it?! Exactly!
There must be a hidden chamber!
It would appear so.
We have to get down there.
Again, there's no "we" here.
EXT. PARIS CATACOMBS OFFICIAL TOUR AREA - DAY
George and Scarlett approach the entrance to the official tourist area of the catacombs where other tourists wait in line. Scarlett buys tickets.
Three tickets please.
Oh, no, I'm not going in. Just two tickets.
Scarlett shoots George a strange look.
Scarlett buys three tickets despite George's reservations.
I'm serious. I'm not going in there.
Don't tell me you're scared of a little cave?
I... have a thing.
What kind of thing?
I don't go underground.
You're an archaeologist!
I know. But I never go underground.
That's kind of sad. It's like being a deaf composer or something.
Regardless, you're not getting me down there.
INT. PARIS CATACOMBS OFFICIAL TOUR AREA - DAY
Benji films Scarlett following the official tour of THE CATACOMBS. Bones and plaques neatly arranged in a morbid but antiseptic display. A French tour guide tours the group of Americans and Brits.

By the 1700s, residents from all over Paris were complaining about the horrible smell of the bodies in the overcrowded cemetery. Something had to be done. Louis the 15th, officially ordered the cemeteries within city limits closed...
... and the remains be brought down here in the old quarries that had been used to build the city.
This place is creeping me out. I don't like being in a tomb.
Scarlett sees a plaque. "Cemetaire des Innocents." She turns and talks to the camera.
These remains are from the same cemetery as the Flamels' graves.
Scarlett references her map.
So, we're looking to go about half a mile right through this wall.

As the tour guide continues, Scarlett cases the structure, like an inmate looking for weak links in a prison. Scarlett finds a small gap between the walls. She looks around. The tour is making a little distance.
I can squeeze through this. Wait for me.
She climbs through the gap in the walls and disappears into the darkness.
Jesus. You kidding me?
(loud whisper) Shine the light in here.
Benji shines a light into the crevasse. Nothing.
He turns the camera back to the tour, still barely out of sight, but coming back. Scarlett?!
BENJI (O.C.) (CONT'D)
He leans the camera in closer to get a better look, completely getting unnerved by this whole thing. SCARLETT LUNGES OUT A THE CAMERA! BAAAH!
BENJI JUMPS BACK AWAY FROM THE HOLE, DROPPING THE CAMERA. Scarlett finds herself pretty funny.
You're going to make me break the damned camera! Sorry.
You are not allowed back there! Please get back into the group!
INT. CATACOMBS TOUR ENTRANCE AREA - DAY
On the way out, the TICKET TAKER looks punk rock cool. He smiles to Scarlett. She approaches him.
(quietly) Do you know any way to get into the private areas of the catacombs?
The Ticket Taker looks around sheepishly.
TICKET TAKER
Thanks anyway.
She notes his Die Antwoord t-shirt.
I saw them in Berlin last April. One of the best nights of my life.
EXT. CATACOMBS ENTRANCE AREA - DAY
George waits outside.
You missed a nice tour. Good.
I didn't see anything that could have...
The Ticket Taker hustles to Scarlett. He hands her a club flyer for Club Silencio.
Ask for Papillon. Thanks.
She nods to him. He nods back and leaves. She holds it up for George to see.
Let's go find Papillon.
EXT. CLUB SILENCIO - NIGHT
Scarlett, George and Benji get out of a cab.
You sure this is the place?
That's what it says. Nothing from the street would indicate this was a club. Just
an average-sized MAN sipping a vodka in front of a nondescript door. Scarlett leads George to the door. The Man stops them. Points to a list.
We're not on the list. He shrugs his shoulders. Not happening.
We're looking for Papillon. MAN Ah, Papillon?!
Why didn't you say so? He opens the door and shows them inside.
INT. CLUB SILENCIO - NIGHT
Scarlett, George and Benji enter a bizarre Parisian nightclub that resembles a beautifully designed bunker. The Man points to the back of the room.
Merci.

She leads George and Benji through dancers and lounge-lizards to some tables in the back. She finds PAPILLON, sipping a red drink, talking excitedly with friends. Had he been born half a century ago, Papillon surely would
times must amuse himself with less constructive forms of
Qui etes-vous?
I need to get into the catacombs. I'm told you could take me.
He laughs.
Do I look like a tour guide to you?
Do I look like a tourist?
He smiles. Takes a sip of his drink.
Why would I want to bring you down there?
She takes a map of the catacombs out of her pocket and slaps it onto the table in front of him.
Because we've found a secret passage that nobody knows about.
Papillon looks at the markings. Shakes his head.
We would have found it if it was there.
No you wouldn't have. It was designed to not be seen.
Why would that be?
Because, I believe, it's full of treasure.
Papillon laughs. Looks to George.
Is she serious?
George nods.
Me and my friends can take half this treasure?
You can have it all. That's not what we're looking for.
Half is probably fine.
You are a strange girl, aren't you.
You don't know the half of it.
INT. PAPILLON'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
Papillon throws a couple pairs of rubber waders and helmets to George and Scarlett. Try these.
Oh, I'm not going in. I'm just here for support.
He's coming. George, I need your help.
This guy seems to know what he's doing.
But who knows what we'll come across down there. I can't decipher it all without you. Come on, we were a good team, remember?
Text it to me and I'll...
No cell phones down there. Ground is too dense.
So there's no way to call for help if something goes wrong? No.
Then I'm definitely not going.
A knock at the door.
(shouts) Entrez! (to Scarlett) My team.

In walks ZED, a quiet, squirrely Frenchman of 25, with large calves and strong hands. A perfectly designed climbing He's trailed by SOUXIE, a motherly Goth girl. She's the den mother of the cataphiles.
This is Zed, the last letter in climbing. And Souxie. The banshee. She sees all the waders and helmets and everything all over the place.
Ah, what a mess Papillon!
She also manages our supplies.
Souxie sorts through the supplies.
INT. PAPILLON'S APARTMENT - LATER
The group stands around a map of the catacombs.
We'll enter through the park. That will make it easier to find La Taupe. La Taupe?
"The Mole."
He lives down there.
He lives down there?! Oui. Weird.
GEORGE
Papillon points to a spot on the map.
He is usually around...
But that's the wrong direction.
Sometimes going the wrong direction is the quickest way.
Do we really need this guy? We have maps and...
Oui. We need this guy.
INT. PAPILLON'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
Souxie counts out all the flashlights, clothes, etc. She tests each flashlight herself. She notices the camera and talks.
It is dangerous underground. If you run out of batteries, and no flashlight, you will die. If you run out of water... If you get hurt.
There are also dangers of cave ins, police, rats, bats, claustrophobia, drowning, bugs.
Many things can go wrong down there. We need to plan for them all. There is no room for mistakes. Papillon checks food and water.
And we always bring enough food and water for a week. A week?!
Papillon shrugs his shoulders.
EXT. WOODED AREA - NIGHT

Scarlett and George follow Souxie, Papillon, and Zed into a dark wooded area in a park in Paris. Papillon stops them about 50 feet shy of a dirt mound with a hole in it, surrounded by concrete and graffiti. The hole looks more like something a raccoon would live in. He and Zed scan the area for police.
The police hate me here. All clear.
EXT. CATACOMBS ENTRANCE - NIGHT
They approach the hole. A tight fit dropping into darkness. Not something you'd ever want to climb into.
You're going in that?! Seriously?
Let's turn on the cameras.
Benji comes around beside Scarlett. He turns on the GoPro attached to her headlamp. He makes a dipshit face into the camera.
B camera, good check.
He approaches Zed, who also has one on his headlamp. Benji flips it on. Makes a dipshit face into camera.
C camera good check.

He looks at it.

Zed climbs swiftly through a jagged shaft of rock to the floor of the catacombs.
EXT. CATACOMBS ENTRANCE - NIGHT
George and Scarlett continue their discussion.
I need you on this.
Scarlett, I'm sorry.

(to George) I don't care what you do, but make up your mind now.

OUT OF NOWHERE, PAPILLON IS TAKEN DOWN RIGHT THROUGH THE CAMERA FROM AN OPEN FIELD TACKLE BY A PARISIAN POLICEMAN. A whistle blows. The camera spins to find a second policeman running toward them.
Allez! Allez! Allez!
Sorry George.

Allez! Allez!

INT. CATACOMBS ENTRANCE - CONTINUOUS
Benji makes his way down though the jagged rocks as Zed points out the safe places to steps. George appears, with ruck sack.
You fuckers. Shit!
Scarlett looks around the catacombs. This is much rougher than the official tourist area, but in its way, much more beautiful. Paintings and graffiti on the walls give it an urban, cool vibe.
This is beautiful.
George looks a bit shell shocked to be down here. Scarlett greets him.
I knew you'd come with us.
Yeah, thanks.
Papillon appears above, dropping like a bat into hell. He hits every wall on the way down.
Allez! Allez! Allez!
Papillon digs furiously in his pockets as they race through the tunnels, fleeing from the police.
Arret! Arret!
Papillon lights up a smoke bomb, drops it. The effect of the gathering smoke is eerie, unsettling.
Papillon leaps over a two foot hole in the floor. Followed by Scarlett, and the rest. He motions into an alcove where they wait for the smoke to clear.
The catacombs make the police nervous. They don't like to go very deep in here.

As the smoke clears, they see:
INT. ARTIST ALCOVE - NIGHT
They are in a kind of artists' gallery. The walls are all painted, beautiful. The police are nowhere to be found.

Ha ha. Police will never catch Papillon! An ARTIST creeps out of the shadows and curses Papillon out for bringing the authorities there. He hates the smoke bombs and despises the tourists that Papillon brought with him.
Cessez de reduire la police ici!
Detendez. Je les ai evites avant
Et cette fumee ruine ma peinture.
Bonne chose que votre peinture est terrible.
Scarlett approaches the Artist's work. It really is beautiful.
C'est tres beau. The Artist appreciates this very much.
Avez-vous vu la Taupe?
Non. Il n'a pas ete par ici.
La Taupe has not been here. We must look for him.
There are over 200 miles of tunnels. You suggesting we wander them aimlessly until we find this weirdo?
Je ne sais pas.
Papillon leads them on.
INT. CATACOMBS CORRIDOR
Papillon leads them through a corridor as Scarlett looks over her map.
(calling out) La Taupe? C'est moi, Papillon.
Wait! We're going in the wrong direction. We need to be going that way.

La Taupe knows every cave.
Scarlett holds up her map.
You think because you see something in a book you know it?!
Papillon smacks the map as he talks.

If you want to follow this map go ahead. I'm going to find La Taupe because these caves are dangerous and you need to know where you're going!
I'm going. Who's with me.
Scarlett. He's right.
Why are we wasting our time with...
You know I don't want to spend an extra minute down here than I have to. But if this guy says he needs La Taupe, I'm inclined to trust him.
He doesn't even know that the guy is down here. Or where he is. Going the slow way is very much against Scarlett's nature.
If the stone is down here, it's been here for six hundred years.
It'll keep for a couple extra hours.
She reluctantly relents.
George turns to Papillon.
Now, don't make me look like an asshole.
As they cross through another room, strange electronic music plays. Come.

Papillon leads them through a slightly larger space. On one side, half a dozen cataphiles sit in what look like stone bleachers cut into the wall. On the other side, a movie -- the old silent version of "Phantom of the Opera" with Lon Chaney. The image has been "remixed" to Aphex Twin, creating an interesting blend of old and new. The movie shows the Phantom descending under the Parisian Opera house into the catacombs.
Our cinema.
George looks at the relaxed Parisians watching a movie down here.
You guys are freaks.
INT. CATACOMBS CORRIDOR
As they cross through a corridor, Papillon points out a small replica of the Bocca della Verita in Rome. (A giant disk of a carved face with an open mouth.) He nudges George, points at it.
According to legend, this statue will bite off your hand if you tell a lie.
George is loosening up a bit.
Let's give it a shot.
He reaches his hand toward it, thinks for a moment.
You might not believe this, but my nipples are perfectly shaped like
little Elvis heads. Early Elvis. George slides his hand into the carved mouth. SENDING BATS SHOOTING OUT FROM THE STATUE!
Aaah! Shit! I hate this place.
Papillon has a good laugh at George.
Yeah, thanks guy.

As they cross to the far end of the corridor, they hear something -A kind of industrial dance music. The beats build as they get closer. Papillon looks to the others, does a little rave-y dance, smiles, and runs toward the music. George and Scarlett look to each other. This fucking guy. The music gets louder as they get closer. As they turn a bend, they see:
INT. CATAPHILE RAVE

AN underground rave, lit in flashing reds and pulsing techno music. People shuffle and dance like the possessed. At a glance, it looks like people might be having sex in the dark corners. Others are scantily clad, in underwear or less. People wearing masks and horns and animal headdresses. The kind of underground party we all know probably happens somewhere, but would never get invited to ourselves. He's wearing nothing but combat boots and his underwear, revealing a swastika on his chest. Scarlett pushes away, but he's aggressive. George jumps forward, hitting the Skinhead across the face, slamming him back through the crowd and onto his back. Other Ravers jump to the Skinhead's aid, striking George in the mouth. Scarlett tries to intervene on George's behalf, but no words can be heard over the pulsing music. People, very tough, very scary people corner George. One takes a swing, but George blocks it. George gets ready for a real fight. He's amped up. Scarlett inserts herself between George and the crowd. Papillon pushes his way through, and holds the ravers off George. Scarlett and George push their way through the crowd and back out of the room.
INT. CATACOMBS CORRIDOR
As they clear the room, the flashlights overpower the strobes. They can hear again. George touches the back of his hand to his mouth and sees the blood.
(to Scarlett) You're welcome.
I didn't thank you.
That guy was all over you.
I could've handled it myself.
Yeah, really?
Those people are crazy people. Dangerous people.
Papillon makes a crazy sign with his hands.
You overreact.
I literally punched a Nazi. How am I suddenly the asshole?
I'm not some damsel in distress. I don't need your help.
George looks around. At himself. In the catacombs.
Yeah? Since when?
(playful) Don't kid yourself. You wouldn't want to go toe-to-toe with me. I'm pretty tough.
You?! That's ridiculous.

Papillon laughs and claps.
I'm not going to push a girl.
Because you're scared of her?
I'm not... this is so stupid. What, you have some self-defense-classkarate-move you learned in case somebody tries to grab you? That's all well and good unless somebody pushes you...
He rushes at her.
...LIKE THIS!
Scarlett steps toward him, nails him in the solar plexus, knocking the wind out of George.
I might know a little more than a self-defense-class-karate-move.
Papillon laughs, raises her hand up in victory.
The champion!
I think he just called you a mushroom.
INT. CATACOMBS MUSHROOM ALLEY
They enter an alcove with bright fluorescent paint on the walls, a lot of it of mushrooms or other hallucinogenicinspired motifs. Papillon calls down a tunnel. La Taupe?
They are all looking a bit tired. They've covered a lot of ground already. A voice calls out from the darkness.
LA TAUPE (O.S.)
Chantez-le? Papillon stops, does a weird little jig.
(sings)
La Taupe, La Taupe, La Taupe. C'est
Papi, Papi, ion, ion ion. From the darkness, a voice sings.
Papi, papi, papi, ion, ion, ion. C'est moi, c'est moi aussi, La Taupe est le garcon!
La Taupe rushes out of the shadows and gives Papillon a bear hug, his face obscured in shadows under his hooded sweatshirt.
Mon ami etrange!
Mon ami puant!
La Taupe looks at Scarlett and George suspiciously.
Qui sont-elles ?
Ils sont ok. Vous ne vous inquietez
Pourquoi ont-ils des appareilsphoto?
Ils font un documentaire environ... Non!
La Taupe covers his face with his hand.
(to the camera) You cannot film me! I do not permit to you filming me.

Go. Get out of these place!
He takes a beer bottle and throws it at them, almost hitting
Whoa!
Papillon holds up a hand. Give me a sec. He walks off with La Taupe.
This doesn't bode well.
George and the gang watch Papillon and La Taupe exchange terse words. After a discussion, Papillon returns.
He will join us. Great.
If we still want him. Guy threw a bottle at my head.
He just threw it. It wasn't at your head.
It skimmed my head. I felt it on my hair.

Sleeps?! Did you say, "first he sleeps?!"
He has eaten the... how you say...
Papillon looks around, points to a graffiti image of a
These. Mushrooms?
GEORGE
Oui. Mushrooms.
He's on mushrooms?! Yes.
How long ago did he eat them?
Two hours.
So he's going to be high for like another six?!
Scarlett looks at him. George shrugs his shoulders. I know
We stay here for the night.
For the night?!
Yes. We sleep.
No. Fuck this. I'm out. I gotta get out of this place. I'm packing my shit up and leaving.
How will you find your way back out?
It's that way.
The camera pans off to show the long, dark corridor, George points at.
Go ahead. Try. We'll send a search party for you in a couple days.
You know, I'm getting real sick of your tone.
I'm sure the skinhead party would love to see you again without me to save you.
You didn't save me.
Why don't you just try to relax.
Because I'm trapped under the ground! In a rat filled tunnel that was dug in the 1500s. Our tour guide is high as a kite and so far we've been walking in the wrong
direction. What could possibly stop
Papillon lights up a joint. Offers some to George.

INT. CATACOMBS MUSHROOM ALLEY

Glow sticks light the space to save flashlights. La Taupe, now wearing a kind of industrial mole mask that he'll continue to wear, spins little flaming balls on strings to music. Papillon drinks beer and talks with Scarlett and La Taupe as Zed practices throwing rocks into a bucket.
You don't seem like a treasure hunter.
I'm not really.

It doesn't make any sense! A rock that can turn other rocks to gold and - AND - can also make you live forever. Doesn't make sense.

Science was different back then. Alchemists were looking for a connection between what was inside them and what they were observing in the outside world. They were trying to perfect themselves, make themselves pure as gold while working in metals. They respected inner truth more than outside facts.
She turns back to Papillon.

I guess I'm not searching for treasure so much as I'm searching for proof of the miraculous. Because if even one alchemist succeeded, if one perfected himself and his own mind and talents and knowledge to the point that he could bend the laws of physics... then we all could. Every one of us. Papillon shrugs. Maybe.
Gold is good too.
Scarlett grabs a couple beers and crosses the room toward George. The camera turns to George, anxiously sitting alone. Scarlett approaches George with a beer.
George shrugs a bit.
Not my favorite place.
So I gathered.
She offers George a beer. He takes it.
When's the last time we had a beer together?
It's been awhile, hasn't it.
You never returned my calls. After Turkey. I know.
I'm sorry I left you there. I know.
I'm glad you're here.
(reluctant) Yeah. Me too. If you're right, this could be the greatest archaeological find... ever. As big as finding the Holy Grail. I know.
George looks around the space.
I used to love exploring caves with my brother as a kid. He was so daring and cool and older. He remembers it all so vividly.
A rock I was standing on dislodged and rolled onto his leg, pinning him. We tried and tried but couldn't get him out. I went to get help, but I got lost trying to find my way back.
This pains George terribly.

That's awful. I'm sorry.
Yeah. It was.
He shrugs.
It wasn't because you left me there.
What's that?
Why I didn't call you back. It wasn't because you left me. It wasn't?
He looks at her seriously.
I was afraid I was falling in love with someone so obsessed with the dead, there's no room left over to love the living.
She takes this in for a moment. Not one to take criticism easily.
And I never thought I'd fall for the kind of guy who runs the second things get complicated.
She turns and goes back to the fire with the others. Shit. Camera powers off.
INT. CATACOMBS MUSHROOM ALLEY

Darkness. Snores. Sleeping people rustling around. Someone moves in the space. BA-BA-BA-BA-BA-BA-BA! Suddenly, the opening chords of the White Stripes, "I Think I Smell a Rat" rip through the darkness. Everyone frantically searches for a light. George is first to get his hands on a flashlight which he flips on and looks for the source of the music. It's La Taupe in his industrial mole mask, blaring a stereo at them, "Say Anything" style. He dances to the music and laughs at their startled reactions. He turns off the music. We go.
LA TAUPE
La Taupe turns and leaves down a corridor, expecting them to catch up.
(under his breath) Asshole.
The bleary-eyed explorers hustle to pack up and follow.
(to Papillon) This "Mole" guy always so annoying?
Papillon shakes his head.
He gets worse.
INT. CATACOMBS CORRIDOR - LATER
La Taupe winds them through corridor after corridor. He knows his way around here like his own neighborhood. George tries to talk with him.
Papillon told me you pretty much live down here?
Down here there is no rules. No law. Here I do whatever I want when I want it.
You do a lot of drugs, don't you. Yes.

I can't imagine a place I'd less want to eat mushrooms. (thinks) Maybe jail. It's a toss up.
INT. BONE TUNNEL - LATER

Careful there are...

(to George) Come closer. Get close.
George crawls over the bones, getting closer behind La Taupe, not sure why Taupe is asking him to do so. La Taupe rips a fart right into George's face, and laughs. George looks back to Scarlett.

The camera moves with Scarlett as she climbs across the bones. She is anxious as the bones creak underneath her. A swarm of rats make noise underneath. The tunnel gets tighter and tighter as they go, to the point that Scarlett is barely thin enough to fit through. Benji gets caught. I'm stuck.
Fat tourist!
(to La Taupe) Arret!
I can't move.

Can you take off your pack?
I already did.

You're making it worse! Stop struggling. Take a breathe.
Ah! The rats are nipping at me. Go! Just go! I need to get out of here!
Benji stop! Stop! You need to back up a bit. You're lodged and...
Fuck! I hate this.
Move back. Just inch back.
No! No! Just...
Benji! The only way forward is
backward! INCH BACK! INCH BACK!
Okay, okay.
He inches back.
There are people behind me too! I just need to get out of here! I don't like this.
Move your right shoulder down and inch back.
Benji moves.
No, no, no, I'm sorry. Your left shoulder.
I'm freaking out. I need to get out.
Keep breathing. Just take a deep breath with me.
No, I need...
Take a deep breath!
Scarlett takes a deep breath and exhales.
Your body expands when your blood pressure rises. You need to calm yourself. Come on.
She does it again. He joins her this time.
It's not helping.
You're going to be okay. Move your right shoulder down an inch. Like this?
That's right. Now see if you can adjust the bones underneath you. Flatten them out, line them up. I...
Just do it. Okay.
His shaking hands arrange the bones.
Ow! A rat just bit me! Ah! Fuck!
It's okay. It's okay, Benji. You're going to be fine. Just follow me.
Benji pulls against the cave. It rumbles slightly.
It's gonna collapse.
Just push! Push through it!
He does. And finally breaks free.
INT. BONE CHAMBER - LATER
They all file out of the bone tunnel.
This is why we don't bring fat Irish tourist to the catacombs.
La Taupe jabs his finger into Benji's gut.
This guy, this fat guy he do not belong here. Fat guy, you should have taken fat man hall.
La Taupe points to another hallway that leads to this same end point. They didn't have to do this to Benji.
We didn't even have to go through there?! Why are you making this harder than it has to be?!
It's more interesting this way.
Scarlett snaps. She pushes him up against the wall.
I don't care who you think you are, but the next time you take a chance with my people, I'll hurt you. Seriously.
La Taupe's mouth, showing under his mole mask, smiles.
English girl has fire.
He nods that he understands, amused by Scarlett's spunk. The ceiling dusts them all a bit.
We shouldn't stay very long.
Was that bad?
They look to see a stone pillar that should be holding up the ceiling has fallen.
That is bad.
We're still not deep enough. We're only two hundred feet below ground. We need to go down another hundred.
The ceiling dusts them again.

(to La Taupe) We need to go deeper.
He reluctantly nods.
LA TAUPE INT. CORRIDOR

La Taupe leads them into the next corridor. He shines his light around until he finds some planks of wood on the ground. He pries them up, revealing a hole in the floor that goes very deep. George looks at it.

One after another, they descend deeper and deeper into the catacombs. They're all feeling the tension of going this deep underground. George looks up to the vast distance above him.

About 6 hours.

Hungry fat Irish.

a three hour tour.

GEORGE, SOPHIE AND BEN
Devenir commencee par temps
pour le courage de l'equipage courageux que le Vairon

PAPILLON, TAUPE, SOUXIE, ZED La terre reglee de bateau sur le rivage de cette ile

professeur et Mary Ann, ici

(bummed) Dommage. I was excited to be rich.
Scarlett scans the surface for a sign, an inscription,
She thinks and thinks. She looks at her map. Mutters to herself, thinking through everything that's led them here, getting more and more frustrated.
There's nothing.
Sorry Scarlett.
I really thought we had it right. I know.
He sees how sad she is.
For what it's worth, I thought we had it right too.
Are we ready to go the other direction?
Yeah...
Scarlett looks down at the compass on her wrist. A flash of insight!
Wait! Wait! Direction! Magnetic north! What?
Magnetic north! True north remains fixed, but magnetic north shifts
about 2.5 degrees per hundred
Which by now would have produced a...
Variation of about 15 degrees. Not here. There!
A second small tunnel leading off the one they're in. They race into it.
INT. SMALL TUNNEL
Scarlett throws her pack down and scans the walls with her fingers. She digs into the stone. Sees something.
Oh my God. There's something there! I know!
She digs and digs.
Don't wreck it. Look!

La Taupe is right, on the wall is a SMALL INSCRIPTION OF A VULTURE.

"...with brightest light, in
darkest day."

"Winged vulture leads your way..."

It's how they sealed pharaohs' tombs in ancient Egypt. The hinges formed a kind of riddle almost like an ancient padlock. There are a series of moves you must get precisely correct. Or what?
Or it'll fall on you and crush you. Or the ceiling would collapse, or something else awful that would probably kill you. Jesus.
She gets ready to give it a shot. The cataphiles cautiously back away, just in case.

She opens her notebook...
What was the... on the tomb...
"Underneath heaven's reign..."

So they still believed everything
rotated around the Earth.

That's my count.
She slides the stone out to the seventh line. The ceiling rumbles as the weight resettles. They look at it. Seems okay.

Now we twist. How far?

... "atop this pair'ed fate."

The stone drops out of place and lands on the floor with a thud, revealing: A SMALL TUNNEL ABOUT 30 FEET LONG, BARELY WIDE ENOUGH FOR A PERSON TO SQUEEZE THROUGH. La Taupe quickly crowds in to look. This is probably the first time someone has shined a light in that space in over 500 years.
A new tunnel.
The treasure.
Give me a boost.

Papillon places his knee so she can use it to climb through. Scarlett climbs into the hole. She barely squeezes through, but keeps going and going and going. Her breathing gets labored as she pulls her way through. From the door side of the hole, we see her clear the other end. She turns back to face the others.

George. Seriously. Come here. George really doesn't want to do this. Shit.

INT. ROSE CHAMBER
George comes out the other side and rolls out of the hole. He stands to find: A tomb, atop which is laid A CORPSE, CLEARLY SEVERAL CENTURIES OLD, PERFECTLY PRESERVED.

C.R. Of R.C.
Christian Rosenkreuz of the Rosy Cross.
